author: m.kalinin
content: <@592004585506340885> what is the problem of not making any spec change and starting all BLSToExecution tests with Capella? In other words, what are we trying to solve by removing this check?

author: tersec
content: but would the test run long enough to enforce either way that it was included in a block by capella, i.e. that it was queued? because that's not what was historically required

author: tersec
content: if it doesn't check, I don't really care, it's just as easy to do either. but if it requires enqueuing this thing which was never historically required to be enqueued pre-capella in a way which requires deferring validation which ... was never required to have been deferred, then that requires, now, years later, building out a system to artificially handle this test scenario in a way which does not map to anything Nimbus actually supports

author: tersec
content: propagate + internally do nothing is just as easy as ignore + internally do nothing for pre-capella, between those two choices I don't care

author: jtraglia
content: The problem is that <@1035747431461167125> told me making these tests work (how they are now) is non-trivial. I think it has to do with the fact that in forks after capella, their fork epoch values are still far future epoch, so the test handler has difficulties determining what the current fork is. I don't truly understand why it's so difficult. I mentioned something about using the manifest to determine what fork is being tested, but that wasn't easy either. So instead of forcing clients to deal with this situation, we wanted to just remove the check so this topic behaves like others.

author: tersec
content: I guess the more principled concern I have is, the tests should be more, not less realistic. And this topic fundamentally does not and cannot behave the same way pre/post capella

author: tersec
content: (see my questions: it can, if and only if one specifically avoids testing anything interesting about it)

author: tersec
content: everything about this feels like a kludgy workaround for ... something, and I'm not yet sure what

author: jtraglia
content: The tests are just unit tests for the gossip validation functions. We definitely would not wait to see if it were included in a block. Skim these to see what we're doing: https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/master/tests/core/pyspec/eth_consensus_specs/test/capella/networking/test_gossip_bls_to_execution_change.py

author: jtraglia
content: Yes, I agree. But that's not how the testing framework was designed. This was before my time.

author: jtraglia
content: Assigning non-far_future_epoch values for forks in minimal & for non-live forks in mainnet is not simple.

author: jtraglia
content: I have worked on this some in the past & gave up because it caused huge problems for existing tests.

author: jtraglia
content: I'm just trying to compromise here for simplicity. If you guys say we should keep this check, that's fine. But we'll need to fix this some other way.

author: tersec
content: to be clear, I don't feel strongly about it, either way, I just don't really understand the full motivation/impetus

author: jtraglia
content: Let's wait for <@1035747431461167125> to wake up. He can give a better answer.

author: tersec
content: this seems worth investigating though, in general and beyond the BLS to execution gossip tests?
